1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ELSE
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ELSE_KEYWORD
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IF
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.LBRACE
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RBRACE
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RPAR
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.THEN
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 23→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 24→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 25→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtBlockExpression
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 28→
 29→/**
 30→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#formatting-control-flow-statements
 31→ */
 32→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 33→    StandardRule(
 34→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 35→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 36→            setOf(
 37→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 38→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 39→            ),
 40→    ) {
 41→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 45→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 46→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 47→        )
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 51→        node: ASTNode,
 52→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 53→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 54→    ) {
 55→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 56→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 61→                if (node.firstChildNode.elementType == IF) {
 62→                    // Allow single line for:
 63→                    // else if (...)
 64→                    return
 65→                }
 66→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 67→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 68→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 69→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 70→                    return
 71→                }
 72→            }
 73→
 74→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 75→            if (autoCorrect) {
 76→                autocorrect(node)
 77→            }
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
 82→        val prevLeaves =
 83→            node
 84→                .leaves(forward = false)
 85→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
 86→                .toList()
 87→                .reversed()
 88→        val nextLeaves =
 89→            node
 90→                .leaves(forward = true)
 91→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
 92→                .toList()
 93→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 94→
 95→        prevLeaves
 96→            .firstOrNull()
 97→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
 98→            ?.let {
 99→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
100→            }
101→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
102→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
103→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
104→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
105→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
106→            prevLeaves
107→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
108→                .forEach(::addChild)
109→            addChild(previousChild)
110→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
111→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
112→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
113→        }
114→
115→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
116→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
117→            node
118→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
119→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
120→        }
121→    }
122→}
123→
124→public val MULTI_LINE_IF_ELSE_RULE_ID: RuleId = MultiLineIfElseRule().ruleId
125→

content.thinking:
Looking at the code and test file, I can see the issue. The problem is that when there's an `else if` statement that already has curly braces around the body (like `else if (condition) { ... }`), the rule is still triggering because it's checking if the first child of the `ELSE` node is a `BLOCK`, but in the case of `else if`, the first child is an `IF` node, not a `BLOCK`.

Let me trace through the logic:
1. When we have `else if (condition) { ... }`, the AST structure is:
   - `ELSE` node containing an `IF` node
   - The `IF` node has a `THEN` block which contains the `BLOCK` with braces

2. The current code at line 56-58:
   ```kotlin
   if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
       return
   }
   ```
   This checks if the first child of `ELSE` is a `BLOCK`. But for `else if`, the first child is `IF`, not `BLOCK`.

3. Then lines 60-65:
   ```kotlin
   if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
       if (node.firstChildNode.elementType == IF) {
           // Allow single line for:
           // else if (...)
           return
       }
       ...
   }
   ```
   This only allows `else if` when there's no newline in the previous sibling. But the issue is when there's already properly formatted `else if { }` on multiple lines.

The problem is that the rule currently handles:
1. `else { }` (BLOCK as first child) - OK
2. `else if (...)` on a single line without braces - OK (lines 60-65)

But it doesn't handle:
3. `else if (...) { }` multiline - This is what's broken!
 
I see the core problem now. When an `else if` statement has its own block with braces, the rule incorrectly tries to enforce additional bracing. The solution is to recognize that the `IF` node inside the `ELSE` node already has proper block structure. We need to modify the validation to skip enforcing braces when the first child of `ELSE` is an `IF` node with its own block.

This means the rule should explicitly check if the first child is an `IF` node and has a properly structured block, and if so, immediately return without attempting to add additional braces. The validation can then separately handle the internal structure of the `IF` statement. The problem lies in the conditional logic for handling `ELSE` nodes with an `IF` child. Currently, the check for skipping brace requirements only occurs when `treePrev` lacks a newline. This means cases with newlines in `treePrev` incorrectly trigger an error for valid `else if` structures.

The solution is to unconditionally return when the first child is an `IF` node, regardless of newline presence. This ensures that nested `if` statements within `else` blocks are processed correctly without unnecessary brace enforcement. By moving the type check outside the newline condition, we prevent false error generation for multi-line `else if` constructs.

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. Let me analyze the code:

1. The rule checks if a `THEN` or `ELSE` node needs braces
2. For `else if (...)` constructs, when the first child of `ELSE` is `IF`, the rule currently only allows this when `treePrev` doesn't contain a newline (line 60-65)
3. The problem is that when there's a multiline if-else-if chain with braces like:
   ```kotlin
   } else if (condition) {
       ...
   }
   ```
   The `treePrev` (which is the whitespace before `else`) DOES contain a newline, so the early return doesn't trigger, and the rule incorrectly emits "Missing { ... }" for the `else` branch.

The fix should be: When an `ELSE` node's first child is an `IF` node, we should ALWAYS return (not emit an error), because:
1. The `else if` construct should not be wrapped in braces to become `else { if (...) { } }`
2. The inner IF's THEN and ELSE branches will be validated separately when the rule visits those nodes

It seems my fix broke some existing tests. Let me analyze the failures:

1. `Given an return-if-statement()` - Test at line 354
2. `Issue 727 - Given a deep nested if-else-if-statement without curly braces()` - Test at line 257

The issue is that my fix is now preventing the rule from checking the inner IF statements when they are part of an "else if" construct. But in those tests, the inner IF statements don't have braces and SHOULD have violations reported.

Let me re-think the logic. The problem is:
1. When we have `else if (...) { }` - correctly braced, we should NOT emit an error for the ELSE
2. When we have `else if (...) return x` - without braces, the inner IF's THEN branch should still get an error

The fix I made returns early for ALL `else if` constructs, regardless of whether the inner IF has braces or not. But the original code's intent was to only allow `else if` when:
- It's on a single line without braces (`else if (...) value`)

The real issue is that the rule is emitting "Missing { ... }" for the ELSE node when the first child is IF. This is wrong because we should never wrap `if` with braces to make it `else { if (...) { } }`.

The validation of the inner IF's branches will happen when the rule visits the inner IF's THEN and ELSE nodes.

Wait, let me re-read the failing tests...

Looking at "Issue 727 - Given a deep nested if-else-if-statement without curly braces()" test:
- The code has many `else if` without braces around the body
- Expected violations are for lines 3, 4, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 18, 19, 21, 23, 25, 26, 28
- These are all violations for the THEN and ELSE nodes where the body needs braces
 
I notice the test is checking specific lint violations. The expected violations seem to differ between the deep nested test and the multiline test. This suggests there might be nuanced differences in how the rule handles various nested if-else scenarios.

The code reveals a complex scenario with multiple conditional branches. Each branch lacks curly braces, which triggers lint warnings. Specifically, the rule focuses on requiring braces for the body of each conditional statement, except for the `else if` condition itself.

The key observation is the precise placement of lint violations, which indicates a subtle implementation detail in the linting rule's logic for handling nested conditionals.

When tracing the rule's behavior, I notice the critical mechanism involves examining THEN and ELSE nodes, not the IF nodes directly. This means the rule checks each branch's structure independently, looking for unbraced code blocks that require encapsulation.

For the specific case at line 5, the rule detects a THEN node without a block, and crucially, both the preceding whitespace and parent node contain newline indicators. This combination signals a need to emit a "Missing { ... }" warning.

The test failures suggest a nuanced interaction between the rule's detection logic and specific code structures involving multi-line conditionals, return statements, and potential edge cases in parsing.

I'll investigate the specific test scenarios, focusing on understanding why the new tests with complex conditional expressions and the existing return-if-statement test are triggering unexpected violations. Running a targeted test will help reveal the precise error mechanism and potential rule implementation gaps.

The goal is to diagnose the underlying parsing or matching logic that's causing these seemingly valid code patterns to be incorrectly flagged by the static analysis rule.

 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment

 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 23→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 24→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 25→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtBlockExpression
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 28→
 29→/**
 30→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#formatting-control-flow-statements
 31→ */
 32→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 33→    StandardRule(
 34→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 35→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 36→            setOf(
 37→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 38→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 39→            ),
 40→    ) {
 41→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 45→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 46→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 47→        )
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 51→        node: ASTNode,
 52→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 53→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 54→    ) {
 55→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 56→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            if (node.elementType == ELSE && node.firstChildNode?.elementType == IF) {
 61→                // The "else if" construct should not be wrapped with braces as "else { if (...) { } }".
 62→                // The branches of the inner IF will be validated separately.
 63→                return
 64→            }
 65→
 66→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 67→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 68→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 69→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 70→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 71→                    return
 72→                }
 73→            }
 74→
 75→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 76→            if (autoCorrect) {
 77→                autocorrect(node)
 78→            }
 79→        }
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
 83→        val prevLeaves =
 84→            node
 85→                .leaves(forward = false)
 86→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
 87→                .toList()
 88→                .reversed()
 89→        val nextLeaves =
 90→            node
 91→                .leaves(forward = true)
 92→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
 93→                .toList()
 94→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 95→
 96→        prevLeaves
 97→            .firstOrNull()
 98→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
 99→            ?.let {
100→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
101→            }
102→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
103→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
104→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
105→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
106→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
107→            prevLeaves
108→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
109→                .forEach(::addChild)
110→            addChild(previousChild)
111→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
112→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
113→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
114→        }
115→
116→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
117→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
118→            node
119→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
120→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
121→        }
122→    }
123→}
124→
125→public val MULTI_LINE_IF_ELSE_RULE_ID: RuleId = MultiLineIfElseRule().ruleId
126→

 72→            }

 77→                .takeIf { it.elementType == ELSE }
 78→                ?.treeParent
 79→                ?.hasBracing()
 80→                ?: false
 81→        val thenBracing = thenNode.hasBracing()
 82→        val elseBracing = elseNode.hasBracing()

127→    }

 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId

 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 28→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.parents
 29→
 30→/**
 31→ * Enforce that single line if statements are kept simple. A single line if statement is allowed only when it has at most one else branch.
 32→ * Also, the branches of such an if statement may not be wrapped in a block.
 33→ */
 34→public class IfElseWrappingRule :
 35→    StandardRule(
 36→        id = "if-else-wrapping",
 37→        visitorModifiers = setOf(RunAfterRule(DISCOURAGED_COMMENT_LOCATION_RULE_ID, ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED)),
 38→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 39→            setOf(
 40→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 41→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 42→            ),
 43→    ),
 44→    Rule.Experimental,
 45→    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
 46→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 47→
 48→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 49→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 50→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 51→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 52→        )
 53→    }
 54→
 55→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 56→        node: ASTNode,
 57→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 58→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 59→    ) {
 60→        if (node.elementType == IF) {
 61→            visitIf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 62→        }
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    private fun visitIf(
 66→        node: ASTNode,
 67→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        val outerIf = node.outerIf()
 71→        val multilineIf = outerIf.textContains('\n')
 72→        val nestedIf = outerIf.isNestedIf()
 73→        with(node) {
 74→            findChildByType(THEN)
 75→                ?.let { visitElement(it, autoCorrect, emit, multilineIf, nestedIf) }
 76→            findChildByType(ELSE_KEYWORD)
 77→                ?.let { visitElement(it, autoCorrect, emit, multilineIf, nestedIf) }
 78→            findChildByType(ELSE)
 79→                ?.let { visitElement(it, autoCorrect, emit, multilineIf, nestedIf) }
 80→        }
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    private fun visitElement(
 84→        node: ASTNode,
 85→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 86→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 87→        multilineIf: Boolean,
 88→        nestedIf: Boolean,
 89→    ) {
 90→        if (!multilineIf) {
 91→            visitBranchSingleLineIf(node, emit)
 92→        }
 93→        if (multilineIf || nestedIf) {
 94→            visitBranch(node, autoCorrect, emit, multilineIf)
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun visitBranchSingleLineIf(
 99→        node: ASTNode,
100→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
101→    ) {
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(BLOCK)
104→            ?.let {
105→                // The then or else block is part of single line if-statement. To enforce that such statements are readable and
106→                // simple, blocks are forbidden entirely in single line if-statements.
107→                emit(
108→                    node.startOffset,
109→                    "A single line if-statement should be kept simple. The '${node.elementType}' may not be wrapped in a block.",
110→                    false,
111→                )
112→            }
113→    }
114→
115→    private fun visitBranch(
116→        node: ASTNode,
117→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
118→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
119→        multilineIf: Boolean,
120→    ) {
121→        if (multilineIf) {
122→            if (node.elementType == IF && node.prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD ||
123→                node.elementType == ELSE && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == IF_KEYWORD ||
124→                node.elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == IF_KEYWORD
125→            ) {
126→                // Allow "else if" on single line
127→                return
128→            }
129→            if (node.elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == LBRACE) {
130→                // Allow "else {" on single line
131→                return
132→            }
133→        } else {
134→            // Outer if statement is a single line statement
135→            if (node.elementType == ELSE && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == IF_KEYWORD) {
136→                // Ignore "else if" as it is reported via another message
137→                return
138→            }
139→        }
140→
141→        with(node.findFirstNodeInBlockToBeIndented() ?: node) {
142→            val expectedIndent =
143→                node
144→                    .treeParent
145→                    .indent()
146→                    .plus(indentConfig.indent)
147→            if (text != expectedIndent) {
148→                emit(startOffset, "Expected a newline", true)
149→                if (autoCorrect) {
150→                    upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedIndent)
151→                }
152→            }
153→        }
154→    }
155→
156→    private fun ASTNode.findFirstNodeInBlockToBeIndented() =
157→        findChildByType(BLOCK)
158→            ?.children()
159→            ?.first {
160→                it.elementType != LBRACE &&
161→                    !it.isWhitespaceBeforeComment() &&
162→                    !it.isPartOfComment()
163→            }
164→
165→    private fun ASTNode.isWhitespaceBeforeComment() = isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() && nextLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true
166→
167→    private fun ASTNode.outerIf(): ASTNode {
168→        require(this.elementType == IF)
169→        return parents()
170→            .takeWhile { it.elementType in IF_THEN_ELSE_ELEMENT_TYPES }
171→            .lastOrNull()
172→            ?: this
173→    }
174→
175→    private fun ASTNode.isNestedIf(): Boolean {
176→        require(elementType == IF)
177→        return findChildByType(THEN)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == IF ||
178→            findChildByType(ELSE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == IF
179→    }
180→
181→    private companion object {
182→        val IF_THEN_ELSE_ELEMENT_TYPES =
183→            listOf(
184→                IF,
185→                THEN,
186→                ELSE,
187→            )
188→    }
189→}
190→
191→public val IF_ELSE_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = IfElseWrappingRule().ruleId
192→

 29→/**
 30→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#formatting-control-flow-statements
 31→ */
 32→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 33→    StandardRule(
 34→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 35→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 36→            setOf(
 37→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 38→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 39→            ),
 40→    ) {
 41→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 45→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 46→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 47→        )
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 51→        node: ASTNode,
 52→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 53→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 54→    ) {
 55→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 56→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            if (node.elementType == ELSE && node.firstChildNode?.elementType == IF) {
 61→                // The "else if" construct should not be wrapped with braces as "else { if (...) { } }".
 62→                // The branches of the inner IF will be validated separately.
 63→                return
 64→            }
 65→
 66→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 67→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 68→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 69→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 70→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 71→                    return
 72→                }
 73→            }
 74→
 75→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 76→            if (autoCorrect) {
 77→                autocorrect(node)
 78→            }
 79→        }
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
 83→        val prevLeaves =
 84→            node
 85→                .leaves(forward = false)
 86→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
 87→                .toList()
 88→                .reversed()
 89→        val nextLeaves =
 90→            node
 91→                .leaves(forward = true)
 92→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
 93→                .toList()
 94→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 95→
 96→        prevLeaves
 97→            .firstOrNull()
 98→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
 99→            ?.let {
100→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
101→            }
102→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
103→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
104→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
105→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
106→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
107→            prevLeaves
108→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
109→                .forEach(::addChild)
110→            addChild(previousChild)
111→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
112→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
113→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
114→        }
115→
116→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
117→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
118→            node
119→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
120→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
121→        }
122→    }
123→}

 29→/**

 32→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 33→    StandardRule(
 34→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 35→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 36→            setOf(
 37→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 38→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 39→            ),
 40→    ) {
 41→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 45→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 46→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 47→        )
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 51→        node: ASTNode,
 52→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 53→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 54→    ) {
 55→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 56→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            if (node.elementType == ELSE && node.firstChildNode?.elementType == IF) {
 61→                // "else if" on same line should not be wrapped with braces as "else { if (...) { } }".
 62→                // The branches of the inner IF will be validated separately.
 63→                // Check if this is truly "else if" (on the same line) by looking at the whitespace
 64→                // between ELSE_KEYWORD and the IF inside ELSE.
 65→                if (!hasNewlineBetweenElseAndIf(node, node.firstChildNode!!)) {
 66→                    return
 67→                }
 68→            }
 69→
 70→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 71→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 72→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 73→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 74→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 75→                    return
 76→                }
 77→            }
 78→
 79→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 80→            if (autoCorrect) {
 81→                autocorrect(node)
 82→            }
 83→        }
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    private fun hasNewlineBetweenElseAndIf(elseNode: ASTNode, ifNode: ASTNode): Boolean {
 87→        // Walk backwards from the IF node to find any whitespace with newlines
 88→        // between the ELSE_KEYWORD and the IF
 89→        var current = elseNode.treePrev
 90→        while (current != null && current.elementType != ELSE_KEYWORD) {
 91→            if (current.isWhiteSpace() && current.textContains('\n')) {
 92→                return true
 93→            }
 94→            current = current.treePrev
 95→        }
 96→        // Also check if there's whitespace with newlines at the start of the ELSE content
 97→        // by looking at leaves before the IF_KEYWORD
 98→        ifNode.leaves(forward = false)
 99→            .takeWhile { it != elseNode && it.elementType != ELSE_KEYWORD }
100→            .forEach { leaf ->
101→                if (leaf.isWhiteSpace() && leaf.textContains('\n')) {
102→                    return true
103→                }
104→            }
105→        return false
106→    }
107→
108→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
109→        val prevLeaves =
110→            node
111→                .leaves(forward = false)
112→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
113→                .toList()
114→                .reversed()
115→        val nextLeaves =
116→            node
117→                .leaves(forward = true)
118→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
119→                .toList()
120→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
121→
122→        prevLeaves
123→            .firstOrNull()
124→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
125→            ?.let {
126→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
127→            }
128→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
129→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
130→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
131→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
132→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
133→            prevLeaves
134→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
135→                .forEach(::addChild)
136→            addChild(previousChild)
137→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
138→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
139→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
140→        }
141→
142→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
143→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
144→            node
145→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
146→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
147→        }
148→    }
149→}

I'm investigating why the code's substring method isn't catching the line break. The method checks if the text starts with "if" and looks for newline characters before the "if". In this case, the substring before "if" is an empty string, which doesn't contain a newline. This means the violation detection is skipping this specific code structure, even though the `if` statement is on a different line from its preceding `else`.

The key insight is that the whitespace containing the actual line break isn't part of the node's text. Instead, it exists as a separate whitespace sibling before the ELSE node. To correctly detect this scenario, I'll use `node.treePrev` to examine the preceding whitespace and determine the line break's location.

 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 28→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtBlockExpression
 30→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtIfExpression
 31→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 32→
 33→/**
 34→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#formatting-control-flow-statements
 35→ */
 36→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 37→    StandardRule(
 38→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 39→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 40→            setOf(
 41→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 42→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 43→            ),
 44→    ) {
 45→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 46→
 47→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 48→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 49→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 50→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 51→        )
 52→    }
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 55→        node: ASTNode,
 56→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 57→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 58→    ) {
 59→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 60→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 61→                return
 62→            }
 63→
 64→            if (node.elementType == ELSE) {
 65→                // For "else if" constructs on the same line, the IF is the content of ELSE.
 66→                // We should NOT wrap it with braces. The inner IF's branches will be validated separately.
 67→                // Only skip if it's "else if" on the same line (no newline between else and if)
 68→                val nodeText = node.text.trimStart()
 69→                if (nodeText.startsWith("if")) {
 70→                    // Check if there's a newline between ELSE_KEYWORD and the IF
 71→                    // This whitespace is the treePrev of the ELSE node
 72→                    val prevSibling = node.treePrev
 73→                    if (prevSibling == null ||
 74→                        prevSibling.elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD ||
 75→                        (prevSibling.isWhiteSpace() && !prevSibling.textContains('\n'))) {
 76→                        // "else if" on the same line - don't wrap
 77→                        return
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 83→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 84→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 85→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 86→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 87→                    return
 88→                }
 89→            }
 90→
 91→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 92→            if (autoCorrect) {
 93→                autocorrect(node)
 94→            }
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun hasNewlineBetweenElseAndIf(elseNode: ASTNode, ifNode: ASTNode): Boolean {
 99→        // Check if there's a newline between ELSE_KEYWORD and IF by checking:
100→        // 1. The whitespace sibling before ELSE (treePrev of ELSE, which is between ELSE_KEYWORD and ELSE)
101→        // 2. Any whitespace at the start of ELSE before the IF
102→
103→        // Check the whitespace between ELSE_KEYWORD and ELSE
104→        val treePrevOfElse = elseNode.treePrev
105→        if (treePrevOfElse != null && treePrevOfElse.isWhiteSpace() && treePrevOfElse.textContains('\n')) {
106→            return true
107→        }
108→
109→        // Check if there's whitespace with newline inside ELSE before IF
110→        // (in case ELSE has whitespace as first child before IF)
111→        var child = elseNode.firstChildNode
112→        while (child != null && child != ifNode) {
113→            if (child.isWhiteSpace() && child.textContains('\n')) {
114→                return true
115→            }
116→            child = child.treeNext
117→        }
118→
119→        return false
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
123→        val prevLeaves =
124→            node
125→                .leaves(forward = false)
126→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
127→                .toList()
128→                .reversed()
129→        val nextLeaves =
130→            node
131→                .leaves(forward = true)
132→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
133→                .toList()
134→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
135→
136→        prevLeaves
137→            .firstOrNull()
138→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
139→            ?.let {
140→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
141→            }
142→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
143→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
144→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
145→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
146→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
147→            prevLeaves
148→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
149→                .forEach(::addChild)
150→            addChild(previousChild)
151→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
152→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
153→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
154→        }
155→
156→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
157→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
158→            node
159→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
160→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
161→        }
162→    }
163→}
164→
165→public val MULTI_LINE_IF_ELSE_RULE_ID: RuleId = MultiLineIfElseRule().ruleId
166→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ELSE
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ELSE_KEYWORD

  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RBRACE
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RPAR

 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 23→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 24→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 25→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtBlockExpression
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 28→
 29→/**
 30→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#formatting-control-flow-statements
 31→ */
 32→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 33→    StandardRule(
 34→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 35→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 36→            setOf(
 37→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 38→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 39→            ),
 40→    ) {
 41→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 45→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 46→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 47→        )
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 51→        node: ASTNode,
 52→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 53→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 54→    ) {
 55→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 56→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 61→                if (node.firstChildNode.elementType == IF) {
 62→                    // Allow single line for:
 63→                    // else if (...)
 64→                    return
 65→                }
 66→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 67→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 68→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 69→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 70→                    return
 71→                }
 72→            }
 73→
 74→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 75→            if (autoCorrect) {
 76→                autocorrect(node)
 77→            }
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
 82→        val prevLeaves =
 83→            node
 84→                .leaves(forward = false)
 85→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
 86→                .toList()
 87→                .reversed()
 88→        val nextLeaves =
 89→            node
 90→                .leaves(forward = true)
 91→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
 92→                .toList()
 93→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 94→
 95→        prevLeaves
 96→            .firstOrNull()
 97→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
 98→            ?.let {
 99→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
100→            }
101→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
102→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
103→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
104→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
105→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
106→            prevLeaves
107→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
108→                .forEach(::addChild)
109→            addChild(previousChild)
110→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
111→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
112→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
113→        }
114→
115→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
116→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
117→            node
118→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
119→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
120→        }
121→    }
122→}
123→
124→public val MULTI_LINE_IF_ELSE_RULE_ID: RuleId = MultiLineIfElseRule().ruleId
125→

 29→/**
 30→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#formatting-control-flow-statements
 31→ */
 32→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 33→    StandardRule(
 34→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 35→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 36→            setOf(
 37→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 38→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 39→            ),
 40→    ) {
 41→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 45→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 46→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 47→        )
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 51→        node: ASTNode,
 52→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 53→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 54→    ) {
 55→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 56→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            if (node.elementType == ELSE && node.firstChildNode?.elementType == IF) {
 61→                // The "else if" construct should not be wrapped with braces as "else { if (...) { } }".
 62→                // The branches of the inner IF will be validated separately.
 63→                return
 64→            }
 65→
 66→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 67→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 68→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 69→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 70→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 71→                    return
 72→                }
 73→            }
 74→
 75→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 76→            if (autoCorrect) {
 77→                autocorrect(node)
 78→            }
 79→        }
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
 83→        val prevLeaves =
 84→            node
 85→                .leaves(forward = false)
 86→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
 87→                .toList()
 88→                .reversed()
 89→        val nextLeaves =
 90→            node
 91→                .leaves(forward = true)
 92→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
 93→                .toList()
 94→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 95→
 96→        prevLeaves
 97→            .firstOrNull()
 98→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
 99→            ?.let {
100→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
101→            }
102→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
103→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
104→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
105→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
106→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
107→            prevLeaves
108→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
109→                .forEach(::addChild)
110→            addChild(previousChild)
111→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
112→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
113→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
114→        }
115→
116→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
117→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
118→            node
119→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
120→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
121→        }
122→    }
123→}

 30→/**
 31→ * All branches of the if-statement should be wrapped between braces if at least one branch is wrapped between braces. Consistent bracing
 32→ * makes statements easier to read.
 33→ */
 34→public class IfElseBracingRule :
 35→    StandardRule(
 36→        id = "if-else-bracing",
 37→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 38→            setOf(
 39→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 40→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 41→            ),
 42→    ),
 43→    Rule.Experimental,
 44→    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
 45→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 46→
 47→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 48→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 49→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 50→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 51→        )
 52→    }
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 55→        node: ASTNode,
 56→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 57→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 58→    ) {
 59→        if (node.elementType == IF) {
 60→            visitIfStatement(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 61→        }
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    private fun visitIfStatement(
 65→        node: ASTNode,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        val thenNode =
 70→            requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(THEN)) {
 71→                "Can not find THEN branch in IF"
 72→            }
 73→        val elseNode = node.findChildByType(ELSE) ?: return
 74→        val parentIfBracing =
 75→            node
 76→                .treeParent
 77→                .takeIf { it.elementType == ELSE }
 78→                ?.treeParent
 79→                ?.hasBracing()
 80→                ?: false
 81→        val thenBracing = thenNode.hasBracing()
 82→        val elseBracing = elseNode.hasBracing()
 83→        if (parentIfBracing || thenBracing || elseBracing) {
 84→            if (!thenBracing) {
 85→                visitBranchWithoutBraces(thenNode, autoCorrect, emit)
 86→            }
 87→            if (!elseBracing) {
 88→                if (elseNode.firstChildNode?.elementType != IF) {
 89→                    visitBranchWithoutBraces(elseNode, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→                } else {
 91→                    // Postpone changing the else-if until that node is being processed
 92→                }
 93→            }
 94→        }
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    private fun ASTNode?.hasBracing(): Boolean =
 98→        when {
 99→            this == null -> {
100→                false
101→            }
102→            this.elementType == BLOCK -> {
103→                true
104→            }
105→            this.elementType == IF -> {
106→                findChildByType(THEN).hasBracing() || findChildByType(ELSE).hasBracing()
107→            }
108→            else -> {
109→                this.firstChildNode.hasBracing()
110→            }
111→        }
112→
113→    private fun visitBranchWithoutBraces(
114→        node: ASTNode,
115→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
116→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
117→    ): Boolean {
118→        emit(
119→            node.firstChildNode.startOffset,
120→            "All branches of the if statement should be wrapped between braces if at least one branch is wrapped between braces",
121→            true,
122→        )
123→        if (autoCorrect) {
124→            autocorrect(node)
125→        }
126→        return true
127→    }
128→
129→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
130→        val prevLeaves =
131→            node
132→                .leaves(forward = false)
133→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
134→                .toList()
135→                .reversed()
136→        val nextLeaves =
137→            node
138→                .leaves(forward = true)
139→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
140→                .toList()
141→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
142→
143→        prevLeaves
144→            .firstOrNull()
145→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
146→            ?.let {
147→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
148→            }
149→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
150→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
151→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
152→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
153→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
154→            prevLeaves
155→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
156→                .forEach(::addChild)
157→            addChild(previousChild)
158→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
159→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
160→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
161→        }
162→
163→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
164→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
165→            node
166→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
167→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
168→        }
169→    }
170→}
171→
172→public val IF_ELSE_BRACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = IfElseBracingRule().ruleId

 30→/**
 31→ * Enforce that single line if statements are kept simple. A single line if statement is allowed only when it has at most one else branch.
 32→ * Also, the branches of such an if statement may not be wrapped in a block.
 33→ */
 34→public class IfElseWrappingRule :
 35→    StandardRule(
 36→        id = "if-else-wrapping",
 37→        visitorModifiers = setOf(RunAfterRule(DISCOURAGED_COMMENT_LOCATION_RULE_ID, ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED)),
 38→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 39→            setOf(
 40→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 41→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 42→            ),
 43→    ),
 44→    Rule.Experimental,
 45→    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
 46→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 47→
 48→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 49→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 50→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 51→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 52→        )
 53→    }
 54→
 55→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 56→        node: ASTNode,
 57→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 58→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 59→    ) {
 60→        if (node.elementType == IF) {
 61→            visitIf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 62→        }
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    private fun visitIf(
 66→        node: ASTNode,
 67→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        val outerIf = node.outerIf()
 71→        val multilineIf = outerIf.textContains('\n')
 72→        val nestedIf = outerIf.isNestedIf()
 73→        with(node) {
 74→            findChildByType(THEN)
 75→                ?.let { visitElement(it, autoCorrect, emit, multilineIf, nestedIf) }
 76→            findChildByType(ELSE_KEYWORD)
 77→                ?.let { visitElement(it, autoCorrect, emit, multilineIf, nestedIf) }
 78→            findChildByType(ELSE)
 79→                ?.let { visitElement(it, autoCorrect, emit, multilineIf, nestedIf) }
 80→        }
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    private fun visitElement(
 84→        node: ASTNode,
 85→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 86→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 87→        multilineIf: Boolean,
 88→        nestedIf: Boolean,
 89→    ) {
 90→        if (!multilineIf) {
 91→            visitBranchSingleLineIf(node, emit)
 92→        }
 93→        if (multilineIf || nestedIf) {
 94→            visitBranch(node, autoCorrect, emit, multilineIf)
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun visitBranchSingleLineIf(
 99→        node: ASTNode,
100→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
101→    ) {
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(BLOCK)
104→            ?.let {
105→                // The then or else block is part of single line if-statement. To enforce that such statements are readable and
106→                // simple, blocks are forbidden entirely in single line if-statements.
107→                emit(
108→                    node.startOffset,
109→                    "A single line if-statement should be kept simple. The '${node.elementType}' may not be wrapped in a block.",
110→                    false,
111→                )
112→            }
113→    }
114→
115→    private fun visitBranch(
116→        node: ASTNode,
117→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
118→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
119→        multilineIf: Boolean,
120→    ) {
121→        if (multilineIf) {
122→            if (node.elementType == IF && node.prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD ||
123→                node.elementType == ELSE && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == IF_KEYWORD ||
124→                node.elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == IF_KEYWORD
125→            ) {
126→                // Allow "else if" on single line
127→                return
128→            }
129→            if (node.elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == LBRACE) {
130→                // Allow "else {" on single line
131→                return
132→            }
133→        } else {
134→            // Outer if statement is a single line statement
135→            if (node.elementType == ELSE && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == IF_KEYWORD) {
136→                // Ignore "else if" as it is reported via another message
137→                return
138→            }
139→        }
140→
141→        with(node.findFirstNodeInBlockToBeIndented() ?: node) {
142→            val expectedIndent =
143→                node
144→                    .treeParent
145→                    .indent()
146→                    .plus(indentConfig.indent)
147→            if (text != expectedIndent) {
148→                emit(startOffset, "Expected a newline", true)
149→                if (autoCorrect) {
150→                    upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedIndent)
151→                }
152→            }
153→        }
154→    }
155→
156→    private fun ASTNode.findFirstNodeInBlockToBeIndented() =
157→        findChildByType(BLOCK)
158→            ?.children()
159→            ?.first {
160→                it.elementType != LBRACE &&
161→                    !it.isWhitespaceBeforeComment() &&
162→                    !it.isPartOfComment()
163→            }
164→
165→    private fun ASTNode.isWhitespaceBeforeComment() = isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() && nextLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true
166→
167→    private fun ASTNode.outerIf(): ASTNode {
168→        require(this.elementType == IF)
169→        return parents()
170→            .takeWhile { it.elementType in IF_THEN_ELSE_ELEMENT_TYPES }
171→            .lastOrNull()
172→            ?: this
173→    }
174→
175→    private fun ASTNode.isNestedIf(): Boolean {
176→        require(elementType == IF)
177→        return findChildByType(THEN)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == IF ||
178→            findChildByType(ELSE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == IF
179→    }
180→
181→    private companion object {
182→        val IF_THEN_ELSE_ELEMENT_TYPES =
183→            listOf(
184→                IF,
185→                THEN,
186→                ELSE,
187→            )
188→    }
189→}

 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace

 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 23→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 24→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 25→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtBlockExpression
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 28→
 29→/**
 30→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#formatting-control-flow-statements
 31→ */
 32→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 33→    StandardRule(
 34→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 35→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 36→            setOf(
 37→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 38→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 39→            ),
 40→    ) {
 41→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 45→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 46→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 47→        )
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 51→        node: ASTNode,
 52→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 53→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 54→    ) {
 55→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 56→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            if (node.elementType == ELSE && node.firstChildNode?.elementType == IF) {
 61→                // The "else if" construct should not be wrapped with braces as "else { if (...) { } }".
 62→                // The branches of the inner IF will be validated separately.
 63→                return
 64→            }
 65→
 66→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 67→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 68→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 69→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 70→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 71→                    return
 72→                }
 73→            }
 74→
 75→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 76→            if (autoCorrect) {
 77→                autocorrect(node)
 78→            }
 79→        }
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
 83→        val prevLeaves =
 84→            node
 85→                .leaves(forward = false)
 86→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
 87→                .toList()
 88→                .reversed()
 89→        val nextLeaves =
 90→            node
 91→                .leaves(forward = true)
 92→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
 93→                .toList()
 94→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 95→
 96→        prevLeaves
 97→            .firstOrNull()
 98→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
 99→            ?.let {
100→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
101→            }
102→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
103→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
104→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
105→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
106→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
107→            prevLeaves
108→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
109→                .forEach(::addChild)
110→            addChild(previousChild)
111→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
112→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
113→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
114→        }
115→
116→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
117→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
118→            node
119→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
120→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
121→        }
122→    }
123→}
124→
125→public val MULTI_LINE_IF_ELSE_RULE_ID: RuleId = MultiLineIfElseRule().ruleId

 34→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#formatting-control-flow-statements
 35→ */
 36→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 37→    StandardRule(
 38→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 39→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 40→            setOf(
 41→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 42→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 43→            ),
 44→    ) {
 45→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 46→
 47→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 48→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 49→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 50→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 51→        )
 52→    }
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 55→        node: ASTNode,
 56→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 57→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 58→    ) {
 59→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 60→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 61→                return
 62→            }
 63→
 64→            if (node.elementType == ELSE) {
 65→                // For "else if" constructs on the same line, the IF is the content of ELSE.
 66→                // We should NOT wrap it with braces. The inner IF's branches will be validated separately.
 67→                // Only skip if it's "else if" on the same line (no newline between else and if)
 68→                val nodeText = node.text.trimStart()
 69→                if (nodeText.startsWith("if")) {
 70→                    // Check if there's a newline between ELSE_KEYWORD and the IF
 71→                    // This whitespace is the treePrev of the ELSE node
 72→                    val prevSibling = node.treePrev
 73→                    if (prevSibling == null ||
 74→                        prevSibling.elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD ||
 75→                        (prevSibling.isWhiteSpace() && !prevSibling.textContains('\n'))) {
 76→                        // "else if" on the same line - don't wrap
 77→                        return
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 83→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 84→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 85→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 86→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 87→                    return
 88→                }
 89→            }
 90→
 91→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 92→            if (autoCorrect) {
 93→                autocorrect(node)
 94→            }
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun hasNewlineBetweenElseAndIf(elseNode: ASTNode, ifNode: ASTNode): Boolean {
 99→        // Check if there's a newline between ELSE_KEYWORD and IF by checking:
100→        // 1. The whitespace sibling before ELSE (treePrev of ELSE, which is between ELSE_KEYWORD and ELSE)
101→        // 2. Any whitespace at the start of ELSE before the IF
102→
103→        // Check the whitespace between ELSE_KEYWORD and ELSE
104→        val treePrevOfElse = elseNode.treePrev
105→        if (treePrevOfElse != null && treePrevOfElse.isWhiteSpace() && treePrevOfElse.textContains('\n')) {
106→            return true
107→        }
108→
109→        // Check if there's whitespace with newline inside ELSE before IF
110→        // (in case ELSE has whitespace as first child before IF)
111→        var child = elseNode.firstChildNode
112→        while (child != null && child != ifNode) {
113→            if (child.isWhiteSpace() && child.textContains('\n')) {
114→                return true
115→            }
116→            child = child.treeNext
117→        }
118→
119→        return false
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
123→        val prevLeaves =
124→            node
125→                .leaves(forward = false)
126→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
127→                .toList()
128→                .reversed()
129→        val nextLeaves =
130→            node
131→                .leaves(forward = true)
132→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
133→                .toList()
134→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
135→
136→        prevLeaves
137→            .firstOrNull()
138→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
139→            ?.let {
140→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
141→            }
142→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
143→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
144→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
145→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
146→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
147→            prevLeaves
148→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
149→                .forEach(::addChild)
150→            addChild(previousChild)
151→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
152→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
153→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
154→        }
155→
156→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
157→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
158→            node
159→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
160→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
161→        }
162→    }
163→}
164→
165→public val MULTI_LINE_IF_ELSE_RULE_ID: RuleId = MultiLineIfElseRule().ruleId
166→
